fields.description:
## Why

Criterion authors configuring project-specific code-review rules currently see a valid overlay
merge but no criterion execution or eval path, a silent failure that is indistinguishable from a
rubric that found no issues.

ADR 0017 and `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` advertise one project-criteria extension surface across
plan review and code review, but the current code-review runtime only executes the static workflow
list and consults packaged decision/eval lookups. A project can therefore configure a net-new
LLM-backed code-review criterion that merges successfully yet never runs end to end.

## What

Complete the generic, project-id-agnostic code-review runtime seam. The child bug
`8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8` owns the reproduced defect and implementation details. This story owns the
delivered capability and compatibility contract: activated project criteria run exactly once,
their repository prompt and eval assets resolve through the CLI, and deterministic Pass 3 uses
repository-effective routing.

## Scope

- Reuse plan review's runner-side dynamic fan-in pattern without adding a workflow DSL field or a
  hard-coded project criterion id. `CodeReviewBatchRunner` uses a named
  `PROJECT_FAN_IN_STEP_ID = "round_a"` constant as a stateless discriminator: activated project
  prompts are appended only for that step, deduplicated against included static physical ids, and
  never appended for `round_b`. No instance flag survives between reviews. A test submits both
  requests to one reusable runner and proves one project-prompt execution per review across the
  complete topology. This runner seam is chosen because it is the existing plan-review pattern and
  preserves the versioned workflow schema unchanged.
- Map a logical `project.<name>` criterion to physical prompt id
  `code-review-project-<name-with-dots-replaced-by-dashes>`; for this epic the physical prompt/eval
  basename is `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries`. Routing JSON continues to use the
  logical id as its key; translation occurs only when prompt/eval assets are dispatched.
- Extend `criteria.ids.criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, *, gate_key="plan_review")` with
  `CODE_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX = "code-review-"`; the default preserves plan-review callers, while
  `gate_key="code_review"` produces the physical project id. Extend calibration as
  `calibrate_criterion(criterion_id, *, repo_root=None, runs=1, gate_key="plan_review")`; the CLI
  passes its resolved gate key, and calibration passes it to the same mapper. Existing
  criterion-preview/editor callers remain
  plan-review-only and retain the default; code-review project assets are manually authored through
  the documented `.rebar/` overlay surface in this epic.
- Resolve project prompt/eval assets from the reviewed repository snapshot. An activated missing or
  malformed asset is a fatal configuration failure: raise a located `LLMError` before model
  execution rather than emitting an empty-findings coverage gap or silently skipping the criterion.
- Extend `criteria eval` preflight to accept a logical id only when it is active in code review's
  repository-effective criteria. Compute plan-review and code-review membership: zero matches keeps
  the existing unknown-id error, one match selects that gate for `calibrate_criterion`, and two
  matches fail with an explicit ambiguous-gate error rather than silently choosing a prompt.
- Extend `_code_review_prompt_id(prompt_id, repo_root)` to recognize the sanitized physical id only
  when its logical inverse is active, then reuse the existing `_run_code_review_case` arm. Eval
  cases use the `diff` key and return the prompt's native `{findings: [...]}` output; they never run
  through the plan-review `_run_criterion_case` / `pass1_chunk` path and require no new solver arm.
- For every project prompt, retain the local physical-to-logical map built during fan-in. On each
  emitted project finding, overwrite `criteria` with `[logical_id]` and set `reviewer_id` to the
  physical prompt id. Pass 3 therefore consumes only logical routing keys regardless of model
  output; built-in finding attribution remains unchanged.
- Preserve the existing behavior and result shape for repositories without a code-review project
  overlay.
- In `code_review_decide`, compute `routing = registry.effective_routing(ctx.repo_root)` once. Bind
  the kernel's threshold callback to `registry.threshold_for(criterion, routing)`—which returns
  both `block_threshold` and `blocking_enabled`—and call
  `registry.nit_suppressed_criteria(routing)` so both deterministic decision inputs use the same
  repository-effective map; no YAML input or LLM step is added.
- Update `docs/llm-framework.md` to describe code-review project-criterion runtime resolution,
  configuration failures, and the eval entry point, completing rather than replacing ADR 0017.

Out of scope: authoring `project.review-phase-boundaries`, changing four-pass decision math,
modifying packaged built-in criteria, or deploying a Gerrit criterion.

## Sequencing and Rollback

The child bug is implemented and closed before this story closes. Downstream criterion authoring
depends on this story. Revert the fan-in, routing lookup, eval resolution, tests, and documentation
together if overlay-absent compatibility regresses; the additive seam has no data migration.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Child bug `8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8` is closed and its change is landed on `main`; verify with
  `.venv/bin/rebar show 8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8` and
  `git log origin/main --oneline --grep='rebar-ticket: 8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8'`.
- [ ] A synthetic activated code-review `project.*` criterion runs exactly once in Pass 1, retains
  its logical id in every finding's `criteria`, and resolves validator-safe repository prompt/eval
  ids. The same reusable runner receives `round_a` and `round_b` requests for two review runs and
  records one project execution per review; verify with
  `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k 'fan_in or eval'`.
- [ ] Pass 3 applies repository-effective threshold, blocking posture, and nit-suppression routing
  for the synthetic id without invoking an LLM; verify with
  `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k routing`.
- [ ] Activated missing/malformed project assets raise a located `LLMError` before model execution,
  while an overlay-absent repository retains its pre-change result; verify with
  `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k 'missing or parity'`.
- [ ] `rebar criteria eval` accepts an activated logical code-review project id, maps it to the
  sanitized physical prompt id, dispatches `diff` cases through `_run_code_review_case`, and rejects
  unknown/inactive/dual-gate ambiguous ids; verify with
  `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k cli`.
- [ ] `docs/llm-framework.md` contains a `Code-review project criteria` section covering Round-A
  fan-in, logical/physical ids, fatal configuration errors, deterministic Pass-3 routing, and the
  `criteria eval` entry point; verify with
  `.venv/bin/python -c 'from pathlib import Path; s=Path("docs/llm-framework.md").read_text(); assert
  all(x in s for x in ("Code-review project criteria", "round_a", "criteria eval",
  "configuration error", "effective routing"))'`.
- [ ] Shared-overlay and four-pass regression suites pass; verify with
  `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_unified_criteria.py tests/unit/test_code_review_evals.py
  tests/unit/workflow/test_code_review_workflow.py && make lint`.
